title: Project Falcon: Expansion Funding Decision
briefing: AeroDyne Industries is a fictional aerospace components manufacturer headquartered in Dayton, Ohio. Founded in 1998, the company specializes in advanced composite materials for commercial and defense aviation. With annual revenues of $2.4 billion and a workforce of 8,500 employees, AeroDyne has established a strong market position in North America and is exploring expansion into Southeast Asian markets. The board of directors has asked the finance team to evaluate a proposed $450 million capital investment to build a new production facility in Vietnam (codenamed 'Project Falcon'). As a Junior Financial Analyst, you are tasked with estimating AeroDyne's weighted average cost of capital (WACC) and applying capital budgeting techniques to recommend whether the project should be funded.
learningObjectives: Calculate the market value and weights of each capital component
Compute the after-tax cost of debt, cost of preferred stock, and cost of equity using CAPM and the dividend growth model
Evaluate a capital project using NPV, IRR, MIRR, payback period, and discounted payback period
Write a professional recommendation memo that integrates quantitative findings with qualitative reasoning
professorNotes: This mission tests WACC and capital budgeting fundamentals.
